evals.prompt: Scaffold a new 'invoices' feature. It should read from and write to the .NET API's invoices endpoint.
evals.expected_output: A src/features/invoices/ folder matching the existing project's conventions (see the quotes feature), with a service that has explicit typed return values and a component with visible loading/error states.
expectations: Creates src/features/invoices/InvoicesService.ts and at least one component under src/features/invoices/components/
The service function's return type is explicit, derived from the generated schema, not untyped/any
Service functions throw the API error rather than returning null on failure, matching the existing QuotesService.ts pattern
The new component visibly handles a loading state and an error state, not a blank screen
Does not add any direct apiClient calls inside the component

evals.prompt: I need a date formatting helper (e.g. 'Jan 5, 2026') that both the quotes feature and a new invoices feature will use. Set that up.
evals.expected_output: Correctly declines to create a new feature folder for a cross-feature utility and places it in src/shared/lib/ instead.
expectations: Does not create a new src/features/<name>/ folder for this helper
Places the helper in src/shared/lib/ instead, per the skill's explicit guidance that cross-feature utilities belong there
Explains why a feature folder isn't the right home for this (it's a utility used across multiple features, not a feature with its own data/UI)
